component.name: Spinner
component.description: An animated arc that tells users content is loading. Indeterminate (no known duration); draws in the current text color so it adapts to any context.
component.note: SVG arc (~75%, round caps) spun via CSS. appearance=inherit → currentColor. Respects prefers-reduced-motion. Synced to Figma '❖ Spinner'.

tokens.color: currentColor (inherits from the parent's text color)
tokens.stroke: 1.5 units in a 16 viewBox (scales with size), round caps
tokens.motion: rotate 360° / 0.7s linear infinite; slows to 2s under prefers-reduced-motion

useCases: Indeterminate loading where progress is unknown
Inside a button while an action is in flight
Centered in a panel/section while its content loads

commonPatterns.name: Panel loading
commonPatterns.description: Large spinner centered in the area whose content is loading.

commonPatterns.name: Inline with text
commonPatterns.description: Small spinner beside a status label.

commonPatterns.name: Color via context
commonPatterns.description: Set color on a wrapper; the arc inherits it.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a spinner when progress IS known
antiPatterns.reason: A spinner is indeterminate; known progress should be quantified.

antiPatterns.scenario: Many spinners on one screen at once
antiPatterns.reason: Multiple spinners create visual noise and competing focal points.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use one spinner for the whole region, or skeleton placeholders.

antiPatterns.scenario: Showing a spinner for very fast (<300ms) operations
antiPatterns.reason: A brief flash of a spinner reads as a glitch.
antiPatterns.alternative: Delay showing it, or skip it for near-instant operations.

antiPatterns.scenario: Hard-coding a color that fights the surface
antiPatterns.reason: The spinner is designed to inherit currentColor.
antiPatterns.alternative: Set color on a wrapping element instead.

interactions.reducedMotion: Animation slows to 2s rather than stopping, so it still reads as 'in progress'

accessibility.label: aria-label defaults to 'Loading' (override per context, e.g. 'Loading results')
accessibility.screenReader: Announced politely via role=status; the SVG is aria-hidden
accessibility.contrastNote: Inherits currentColor; ensure the inherited color meets ≥3:1 against its background (it is a non-text graphical indicator).

figmaDivergences.figma: 8 discrete keyframes of a rotating arc image
figmaDivergences.implementation: a single SVG arc rotated continuously via CSS
figmaDivergences.reason: Smoother, resolution-independent, and tiny; the visual result matches.

aiHints.context: Reach for Spinner to indicate indeterminate loading — in a button, a panel, or inline. Use Progress bar when progress is measurable. Keep one spinner per region; set color on a wrapper since the arc inherits currentColor.
neverUseWhen: Progress is known/measurable — use Progress bar
The operation is near-instant (<300ms) — skip or delay it
You need many at once — prefer one regional spinner or skeletons
